architecture.summary: Bridge networks combine information bottleneck theory with vector-symbolic architectures to create an information processing system that relates inputs through vector-symbolic manipulations. The architecture aims to address global loss dependencies and catastrophic forgetting by processing information through structured symbolic representations rather than end-to-end gradient flows.

biblio.title: Bridge Networks: Relating Inputs through Vector-Symbolic Manipulations

claims.statement: Bridge networks provide unique advantages which can address the problem of global losses and catastrophic forgetting
The architecture provides a basis for increasing energy efficiency of execution
The architecture provides a basis for the ability to reason symbolically
